page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.2xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. This instance features 8 dedicated physical ARM64 cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The CPU includes 64 KB L1D cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 474 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. Network performance is anchored by a 4 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with a Passmark memory score in the top 10% and a large-block memory write speed of 84.13 GB/sec placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench text processing also demonstrate strong performance in the top 25%. Conversely, cryptographic performance is a relative bottleneck, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 25%. Standard compute benchmarks, including stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and Redis SET operations, consistently register in the middle 50% average tier.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and integrated 474 GB NVMe SSD, the m8gd.2xlarge offers a resource-dense configuration for general-purpose workloads. The dedicated ARM64 cores and high memory bandwidth make it highly efficient for database operations, static web serving, and text processing tasks. However, its lower cryptographic performance and poor prompt processing speeds on larger models suggest it is less suited for heavy encryption tasks or complex machine learning inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8gd.2xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 8 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory performance, with a Passmark memory score in the top 10% and strong large-block memory write speeds. While single-threaded text processing and bzip3 decompression perform in the top 25%, cryptographic operations represent a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and local NVMe storage, the m8gd.2xlarge is well-suited for database operations, web serving, and general-purpose application hosting, though it is less optimal for intensive cryptographic workloads or large-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.2xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server featuring 8 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 32 GB RAM, 474 GB local NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier memory performance.

tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
32.0 GB system memory with top-tier memory bandwidth performance
474 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD instance storage
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong performance in text processing and single-threaded decompression
Suited for database operations, web serving, and general-purpose workloads
